summary: The Market/analytics plane is the least-implemented of AFI's five planes: across the focus repos (afi-benchkit, afi-econ, afi-reactor, afi-ops) there are ZERO warehouse, data-lake, stream, or feature-plane integrations. The only persistence that exists is the per-signal evidence store — MongoDB TSSD (Theme D), which is AFI's canonical reference evidence store. The reactor's only downstream egress hook (ObserverNode.publishSignal) is a no-op placeholder. The portable-protocol spec's separate continuous 'feature schema / snapshot ref' analytics plane is unbuilt and explicitly non-normative; benchkit/econ analytics are file-based, hash-stamped research artifacts with no signalId/epoch linkage to the evidence store. No separate warehouse/stream plane is planned for the reference implementation — afi-reactor + Mongo TSSD is the reference spine.

answers.question: F.20 — Are there any warehouse, stream, or analytics-feature-plane integrations?
answers.answer: No. There are no implemented warehouse, data-lake, stream, or feature-plane integrations in any focus repo. A workspace search for analytics/warehouse/stream egress across afi-benchkit, afi-econ, afi-reactor and afi-ops finds none in code: the reactor's only egress hook, ObserverNode.publishSignal(), is an explicit no-op placeholder (its body only console.logs; the real publish call is commented out). The only persistence that exists is the per-signal MongoDB TSSD evidence store (afi-reactor writes scored signals via tssdVaultService.insertSignalDocument -> collection.insertOne; afi-gateway upserts via afi-infra MongoTSSDVaultClient). The portable spec lists warehouse/stream engines only as NON-NORMATIVE, other-operator options in the Market/analytics plane row, and afi-labs' MVP plan explicitly says NOT to build streaming (use polling or a simple cron to simulate). No separate warehouse plane is planned for the reference implementation.

answers.question: F.21 — Where would a non-reactor (custom) analytics orchestrator fit without violating the orchestration doctrine?
answers.answer: A non-reactor orchestrator may sit in the optional Market/analytics plane (continuous context at scale — feature computation, research, log pipelines) which is NOT per-signal canon, OR act as a Scoring-DAG implementation ONLY if it emits conforming artifacts with pinned topology and plugin/analyst/validator versions — because protocol law is 'conforming DAG outputs + pinned versions', not the reactor binary (the spec's tension resolution reframes afi-reactor as the 'reference orchestrator'). It must NOT replace the canonical per-signal ingest->enrich->score->persist path under the doctrine as written. The reactor doctrine still asserts afi-reactor as the only orchestrator (README, Commandments 1 and 3), which tensions the portable spec's pluggable Scoring-DAG plane; this is tracked as the reactor-only contradiction below and in Theme E.

answers.question: F (separation) — How are per-signal operational stores separated from the analytics / feature planes?
answers.answer: The separation exists only as doctrine in the spec, not as implemented infrastructure — because the analytics plane does not exist. The portable spec's 5-plane model distinguishes the Evidence plane ('Dense per-signal lifecycle for replay and challenge'; Mongo/PostgreSQL/TimescaleDB/InfluxDB) from the Market/analytics plane ('Continuous context at scale (not per-signal canon)'), with the key rule 'Do not collapse commitment, evidence, and analytics into one store.' In practice only the per-signal MongoDB evidence store exists: the canonical afi-infra TSSD VaultedSignalRecord plus reactor's reactor_scored_signals_v1 collection (Theme D), and afi-ops provisions Mongo only (vault backend limited to 'memory' or 'mongodb'; codex marks mongodb required:true). Because no analytics/feature plane is implemented, the planes are not so much 'separated' as the analytics plane simply does not exist; the only data layer is the operational per-signal Mongo store.

answers.question: F (analytics tooling) — What analytics / feature tooling actually exists, and is it wired as a streaming/feature plane?
answers.answer: Two repos produce analytics-like outputs but as file-based research/benchmark artifacts, NOT a wired warehouse/stream/feature plane. afi-benchkit emits hash-stamped benchmark artifacts (scores.json, metrics, plots) via a provenance Stamp dataclass that captures data_hash/cfg_hash/git_sha/utc_ts but NO signalId/epoch/content-hash protocol linkage (grep for signalId|epoch in stamp.py = 0 hits), so its analytics are decoupled from the per-signal evidence vault. afi-econ then consumes afi-benchkit's scores.json as a local sibling file (find_sibling_repo / _load_benchkit_scores) to weight gauge allocation in a research simulation. Neither streams, publishes to a warehouse, nor exposes a feature-store surface; both are SUPPORTING/RESEARCH tooling that maps to the spec's 'benchkit, research tooling' analytics-context row, not to an implemented feature plane.

findings.title: Market/analytics plane is entirely unimplemented; only the per-signal MongoDB TSSD evidence store exists
findings.evidence: afi-docs/specs/AFI_PORTABLE_PROTOCOL_SURFACE.v0.1.md:54 'Market / analytics ... warehouses/streams chosen per operator ... non-normative, never AFI's evidence store'; the only persistence in code is Mongo TSSD (afi-reactor/src/services/tssdVaultService.ts:112 collection.insertOne; afi-infra/src/tssd/MongoTSSDVaultClient.ts:120 upsert)
findings.recommendation: Document the analytics plane explicitly as optional/non-normative in the normative register; do not present any warehouse/stream engine as an available AFI integration. Carry into AFI_PROTOCOL_SURFACE_AUDIT.md per-plane coverage (analytics plane = 0% implemented; evidence plane = Mongo TSSD).

findings.title: Reactor's only downstream egress hook (ObserverNode.publishSignal) is a no-op placeholder, so no analytics/stream egress exists
findings.evidence: afi-reactor/src/dag/nodes/ObserverNode.ts:248-250 'This is a placeholder implementation'; afi-reactor/src/dag/nodes/ObserverNode.ts:260-275 publishSignal body only console.logs, the real downstream publish call is commented out
findings.recommendation: Either implement a conforming analytics egress adapter or mark publishSignal as an intentional extension point in docs; the spec's optional 'snapshot refs for replay' analytics plane currently has no producer, which is acceptable since it is non-normative.

findings.title: benchkit/econ analytics artifacts have no signalId/epoch linkage to the per-signal evidence vault
findings.evidence: afi-benchkit/src/afi_benchkit/stamp.py:14-19 Stamp(data_hash, cfg_hash, git_sha, utc_ts) with no signalId/epoch (grep signalId|epoch in stamp.py = 0 hits); afi-econ/src/afi_econ_kit/cli.py:451 'bench_data = _load_benchkit_scores(...)' consumes scores.json as a sibling file
findings.recommendation: If benchkit/econ outputs are ever promoted into an optional analytics plane, add signalId/epoch/content-hash linkage and a published feature schema so analytics snapshots are replay-referenceable; until then keep them tagged as research tooling.

contradictions.doc_says: afi-reactor is the ONLY orchestrator in AFI Protocol; all canonical pipelines/DAGs live here and the DAG is law (ad-hoc flows are anti-patterns).
contradictions.code_does: The portable spec permits 'any conforming orchestrator' in the Scoring-DAG plane and HTTP analogy, and the spec's own tension table reframes reactor as merely the 'reference orchestrator' whose law is conforming outputs + pinned versions.
contradictions.evidence: afi-reactor/README.md:137 'afi-reactor is the ONLY orchestrator in AFI Protocol'; afi-reactor/docs/AFI_ORCHESTRATOR_DOCTRINE.md:38-40 'The DAG is law'; afi-docs/specs/AFI_PORTABLE_PROTOCOL_SURFACE.v0.1.md:53,68; afi-docs/specs/AFI_PORTABLE_PROTOCOL_SURFACE.v0.1.md:133

contradictions.doc_says: Do not collapse commitment, evidence, and analytics into one store; a single-store design (whatever the engine) is wrong if it is the only layer.
contradictions.code_does: Only the per-signal Mongo evidence store exists; there is no separate analytics/feature plane, and afi-ops provisions Mongo only (vault backend limited to memory|mongodb, codex marks mongodb required:true), so every persisted artifact lives in the operational Mongo evidence layer.
contradictions.evidence: afi-docs/specs/AFI_PORTABLE_PROTOCOL_SURFACE.v0.1.md:57 'Do not collapse commitment, evidence, and analytics into one store'; afi-ops/configs/env.template:40-44 'Vault backend: "memory" or "mongodb"'; afi-ops/.afi-codex.json:93-100 'mongodb ... required: true'
